Cruella is, and always has been, a metaphor for an "adoptress," that’s uses adoption to steal and abuse children, particularly children of mixed race. The actual Walt Disney, was brilliant in playing her as the devil come to life, and probably did so, because he came from a state, Missouri, that has a long history of exploiting children in this way, along with its neighboring states of Kentucky and Tennessee, where the original noted stealer of children through adoption, Georgia Tann, was from, who was clearly the inspiration for Cruella. 

How Disney thinks it’s appropriate, in the middle of a national crisis of race relations, to release a movie that glorifies one of its former villains, who symbolizes the practice of misusing adoption to steal and exploit children, particularly those of color or mixed race, is beyond me, but I’m canceling my Disney+ subscription. What’s next, a rerelease and glorification of “Song of the South?”

It seems like there is this idiotic drive by feminists nowadays, to bring back female villains and play them as “not so bad.”  However, they were so bad. The wicked witch of the West symbolizes a manipulative narcissist, psychopath. Those people are bad, regardless of gender. Cruella Deville symbolizes a narcissistic adoptress, psychopath, that misuses adoption to steal and exploit kids. Those people exist, and they’re monsters! They’re not monster because they’re women, they’re monsters because they’re bad people. Bringing them back as “hero’s to be celebrated,” is as stupid as it would be to bring back Captain Hook, or prince John, from Robin Hood, in that role. 

If Disney wants to make movies about wonderful women, its should make them, there are plenty of them. But it’s really foolish and offensive for it to bring back female villains, particularly racist ones like Cruella, and try to play them as “hero’s now, because they’re female.”

It's an origin of story of a villainess. Every Disney villain had a twisted mature to them. The evil Queen tricked snow-white into biting a poisoned apple making her fall into a sleep coma, Maleficent cursed a new born baby so she would die, Ursula tricked a teenage mermaid into giving up her voice, Jafar wanted to be ruler or the universe, Scar killed his brother to be leader of the pack and tried to kill his nephew too. Everyone had a reason and everyone was evil. You can't possibly think you were gonna watch another Disney movie where love triumphs as always, that's boring. We know how those movies end. I think this is better cause not only do we see her a different light but we see her slowly turning into who she became. I'm not saying it's right but it's just a different look for them, and growing up hating the villain's as a child, I always did wonder why they were evil to start. Sorry for bragging on, it's just something to keep an open mind about. I'm not saying we should all be evil for what we've been thru, I'm saying due to these stories we've grown up to watch, deep down inside some of us always wondered why they did what they do and I'm glad Disney is actually giving us a reason to their stories.
